name: Ned Land
description: Ned Land, the harpooner, would be rendered in a vibrant yet grounded watercolor style. His face, perpetually tanned a deep ochre with hints of burnt sienna, is weathered and etched with fine lines from years at sea. His nose, slightly crooked as if broken long ago, is a focal point, dabbed with a touch of carmine to indicate sunburnt skin. Deep-set eyes, a piercing cerulean blue, are framed by thick, salt-and-pepper eyebrows, rendered with individual, feathery brushstrokes of Payne's Grey and Indigo. A thick, walrus mustache, a messy blend of grey and brown, droops over his top lip, stained faintly with tobacco. His clothing is practical and functional, depicted in muted greens and browns: a canvas jacket, patched and faded with seawater, its collar upturned against the wind; thick woolen trousers in a shade of deep moss, tucked into well-worn, knee-high leather boots, stained with splashes of sepia and umber. Around his neck, a thick, knotted rope serves as a makeshift scarf, painted in a rough texture of greys and faded blues, adding to his rugged, seafaring aesthetic.
